name: RocksDict (Python RocksDB Interface)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Congyuwang; RocksDB itself is Apache-2.0 / GPLv2

performance.notes: RocksDB is a high-performance LSM-tree KV store; microsecond-level point lookups

agent_readiness.notes: Python dict-like interface to RocksDB. from rocksdict import Rdict. db = Rdict('/path/to/db'). db['key'] = 'value'. db['key']. del db['key']. 'key' in db. db.close(). Iteration: for k, v in db.items(): pass. Prefix scan: for k, v in db.items(prefix='prefix'): pass. WriteBatch: wb = db.write_batch(); wb.put(b'k', b'v'); db.write(wb). Column families. Snapshots: snapshot = db.snapshot(). Options: Rdict(path, options=Options(raw_mode=True)). raw_mode=True for bytes keys/values. Serialization: uses msgpack by default for Python objects. Compaction, TTL, bloom filters via Options.

gotchas: Requires compiled Rust bindings — install may fail on unsupported platforms
raw_mode=True required for bytes keys — default uses msgpack serialization
No concurrent multi-process writes (RocksDB single writer constraint)
Column families add complexity — single CF sufficient for most use cases
RocksDB WAL + SST files must be managed (disk space, compaction)
best_when: High-throughput local key-value storage needing better than LMDB or SQLite
Billion-scale KV pairs with LSM-tree write optimization
When LevelDB (python-leveldb) is too slow or limited
Embedded KV store for data-intensive Python applications
avoid_when: Small datasets — SQLite or TinyDB is simpler
Multi-process concurrent writes — need separate server
When LMDB suffices (simpler, faster for read-heavy workloads)
summary: RocksDict provides a Python dict-like interface to RocksDB (LSM-tree KV store). Rdict('/path'). db['key'] = value. Iteration with prefix scan. WriteBatch for bulk writes. Column families. Snapshots. Rust-compiled. MIT by Congyuwang. Best for high-throughput local KV storage beyond SQLite. Single writer. raw_mode for bytes. Manages SST/WAL files. Overkill for small datasets.
